aids.title: Node = DN (Data + Next)
aids.content: Every node has **D**ata and **N**ext. Think of a train: each car (node) carries cargo (data) and connects to the next car (next pointer).

aids.title: Array vs Linked List
aids.content:
**Array** = Apartment building (units are adjacent, easy to find by floor number)
**Linked List** = Treasure hunt (each clue tells you where the next clue is)
aids.forRemembering: Difference between arrays and linked lists

aids.title: LL Complexity Rule
aids.content:
Access = **O(n)** (must traverse)
Insert/Delete at head = **O(1)** (no shifting)

Remember: Linked Lists are SLOW to find, FAST to modify at known position.
aids.forRemembering: Time complexity of linked list operations

aids.title: Never Lose the Head!
aids.content: Head is like your house key - lose it and you can't access anything inside! Always keep a reference to head before traversing.

aids.title: SDCC - Linked List Types
aids.content: **S**ingly, **D**oubly, **C**ircular, **C**ircular Doubly. Remember: 'SDC Computers' - Start with Simple, add features!
